name: Xtrium Materials Graph Schema v1.0 (NASA TPSX)
extraction_instructions:
SOP-DS-001 v1.3 — NASA TPSX Open Materials Database

ONE JSON FILE PER MATERIAL RECORD. Each CSV row = one material = one JSON output file.

CANONICAL NAME: lowercase, spaces to hyphens, remove specials. 'Aluminum 6061-T6' → 'aluminum-6061-t6'

GRADE: Extract ONLY if there is an unambiguous standalone grade code. 'Aluminum 6061-T6' → 'T6'. 'PEEK 450G' → '450G'. 'Phenolic Impregnated Carbon Ablator' → null.

MATERIAL TYPE MAPPING:
- Metals → metal
- Polymers → polymer
- Ceramics → ceramic
- Composites → composite
- Elastomers → elastomer
- Adhesives → adhesive
- Glasses → glass
- Other / unlisted → null (record original Category in processing_notes)

SUPPLIER_CANONICAL_NAME (from Database_Source):
- TPSX → nasa-tpsx
- K&K Associates Materials → kk-associates-materials
- NASA JSC PathFinder Materials → nasa-jsc-pathfinder-materials
- NASA Langley Research Center Database → nasa-langley-research-center-database
- NASA Ames Thermal Protection Materials → nasa-ames-thermal-protection-materials

URL RULE: Copy the URL column value as-is into ALL URL fields. Do not modify.

PROPERTY GROUPING: Columns sharing a base name (Density_Value, Density_Units, Density_Source) belong to ONE property object. Numbered instances (Thermal_Conductivity_1_Value, Thermal_Conductivity_2_Value) = separate objects ONLY if the same base property appears more than once in the row. Count-based labelling: 'Instance 1', 'Instance 2'...

STP → condition MAPPING:
- 'Yes' or 'TRUE' → 'STP'
- 'No' or 'nonstp' → 'Non-STP'
- 'assumed/assumed' → 'assumed STP'
- absent/blank → null
With instance: append both e.g. 'Non-STP, Instance 1'

SOURCE_NAME TWO-LEVEL RULE:
- properties[].source.source_name: use _Source column value if it is a named standard (ASTM, ISO, NASA-TM). Otherwise use 'NASA TPSX'
- sources[0].source_name: ALWAYS 'NASA TPSX Materials Database' (long form)

PROCESSING_NOTES: Combine non-empty sections: 'Composition: {val}\n\nDescription: {val}\n\nNotes: {val}\n\nTRL: {val}\n\nManufacturer: {val}'. Omit any section whose cell is empty, 'N/A', 'n/a', '-', em-dash, 'None', 'Unknown', or 'Description:'.

RAW DICT RULE: If a CSV cell is empty or a suppressed artefact, OMIT that key from raw entirely. Never include a key with null value.

CHEMICAL_FORMULA: Extract ONLY if the entire Composition cell is a formula with no prose. Mixed cells → null, put full cell in processing_notes.

NO DATA LOSS: All 1,287 CSV columns must appear somewhere in the output. Unknown property columns → add to properties[] using the same pattern.

FIXED VALUES:
- material_id: null
- cas_number: null
- uns_number: null
- icsd_id: null
- inchikey: null
- external_id_type: null
- is_variant: false
- base_material_canonical_name: null
- hazard_flags: []
- availability_notes: null
- cross_graph_material_id: null (in all property objects)

fields.name: material_name
fields.description: REQUIRED. Copy exactly from NASA Material_Name column. e.g. 'Aluminum 6061-T6'

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Derived from material_name: lowercase, spaces to hyphens, remove specials. 'Aluminum 6061-T6' → 'aluminum-6061-t6'

fields.name: chemical_formula
fields.description: Extract ONLY if Composition cell contains a formula with NO prose. Mixed cells → null (put full cell in processing_notes). e.g. 'Ti-6Al-4V' → 'Ti-6Al-4V'

fields.name: material_type
fields.description: From NASA Category column. Metals→metal, Polymers→polymer, Ceramics→ceramic, Composites→composite, Elastomers→elastomer, Adhesives→adhesive, Glasses→glass, Other→null. For unlisted categories apply closest match or null. Always record original Category in processing_notes.

fields.name: material_subtype
fields.description: From Composition/Description if clearly stated (thermoplastic, thermoset, reinforced). Otherwise null.

fields.name: grade
fields.description: Only if unambiguous standalone grade code. 'Aluminum 6061-T6' → 'T6'. 'Titanium Grade 5' → 'Grade 5'. 'Phenolic Impregnated Carbon Ablator' → null.

fields.name: trade_names
fields.description: Array of trade names if mentioned in Description/Name. Default: []

fields.name: supplier_context
fields.description: Object: {supplier_canonical_name: 'nasa-tpsx' (see mapping), supplier_product_id: null, supplier_grade_name: grade value from material_name, datasheet_url: URL column value as-is}

fields.name: properties
fields.description: Array of property objects — one per property group per instance. Each: {name: 'Density', canonical_name: 'density', value: '2.7' (string), numeric_value: 2.7 (float), unit: 'g/cm³', condition: 'STP'|null, property_category: 'physical', raw: {Value:..., Units:..., Source:...}, source: {source_name: 'NASA TPSX', source_url: URL, doi: null, tier: 'tier1'}}

fields.name: standards_referenced
fields.description: Parse ASTM, ISO, NASA-TM codes from Property_References. e.g. ['ASTM D638', 'ISO 527-1']. Default: []

fields.name: processing_notes
images
fields.description: From Pictures column. Each URL → {image_url: '...', image_data: null, image_type: null}. Empty Pictures → []

fields.name: sources
fields.description: Always: [{source_name: 'NASA TPSX Materials Database', source_url: URL column value, doi: null, tier: 'tier1', raw: {General_References: ..., Point_of_Contact: ..., Last_Modified: ...} — omit raw keys whose cells are empty or suppressed artefacts}]
